name: UnrealClaudeMCP
description: Drive Unreal Engine 5.7 from Claude (or any MCP client) over a local TCP socket. Thirty-two generic editor-automation tools that work with any UE 5.7 project.

tools.name: execute_unreal_python
tools.description: Run arbitrary unreal.* Python in the editor's embedded interpreter (universal escape hatch). Multi-line scripts work.

params.code: string (required) - Python source code to execute

tools.name: get_project_summary
tools.description: Project name, engine version, enabled plugins, asset counts.

tools.name: inspect_blueprint
tools.description: Variables, function/event graph names, parent class of a Blueprint asset.

tools.name: inspect_widget_tree
tools.description: Read the widget hierarchy of a UWidgetBlueprint or EUW. Bypasses UE 5.7 Python reflection limit on WidgetTree.

tools.name: edit_widget_tree
tools.description: Mutate a widget tree. ops: set_root | add_child | set_property. Solves UE 5.7 EUW WidgetTree population.

tools.name: get_viewport_screenshot
tools.description: Capture the active editor viewport as base64 PNG.

tools.name: list_tools
tools.description: Return the names of every registered MCP method on the server.

tools.name: get_actors_in_level
tools.description: Return name/class/transform of every actor in the active editor world.

tools.name: focus_actor
tools.description: Select an actor by label/name and frame the viewport on it.

tools.name: load_level_by_path
tools.description: Load a UE level by package path.

tools.name: take_high_res_screenshot
tools.description: Trigger UE's HighResShot. Output to Saved/Screenshots/<Platform>Editor/.

tools.name: import_texture
tools.description: Import an image file (PNG/JPG/EXR/TGA/BMP/HDR) from disk into the project as a UTexture2D asset, using the canonical UE asset import pipeline.

params.dest_path: string (required) - UE package path starting with /Game/

tools.name: configure_texture
tools.description: Adjust SRGB/CompressionSettings/LODGroup/Filter on an existing UTexture asset and persist the change. Triggers UE's standard PreEditChange/PostEditChange flow and rebuilds the GPU resource.

params.path: string (required) - UE package path of an existing texture

tools.name: find_assets
tools.description: Query the asset registry by class + optional path + optional name substring + optional tag filters. Returns matching assets with structured records (name, package_path, class[, tags]).

params.tags: object (optional) - map of tag-name -> exact-match string or null (any value); AND-combined
params.include_tags: bool (optional, default false) - when true, each result asset includes a 'tags' map

tools.name: spawn_actor
tools.description: Create an actor in the current editor world at a location with optional rotation, label, and initial properties. Class path can be built-in (/Script/Engine.StaticMeshActor) or Blueprint (/Game/Blueprints/BP_X.BP_X_C).

tools.name: set_actor_transform
tools.description: Move / rotate / scale an existing actor by name (label or FName). Supports both absolute and relative modes.

tools.name: delete_actor
tools.description: Remove an actor from the editor world by name (label or FName). Children are detached, not destroyed (UE's default behavior). Force flag overrides the children-attached safety check.

tools.name: set_actor_property
tools.description: Mutate any UPROPERTY on an actor. v0.4.0 supports primitives, all common UE structs, enums, TSoftObjectPtr, plus USTRUCT (recursive)/TArray/TMap (string-keyed)/TSet/FObjectProperty (hard UObject pointers via asset path). Property names accept dotted-path syntax for nested traversal (e.g. 'RootComponent.RelativeLocation'). FInstancedStruct deferred to v0.4.x.

tools.name: add_component
tools.description: Attach a component (UActorComponent or USceneComponent subclass) to an existing actor at runtime, optionally socketed and transformed relative to a parent component.

params.actor_name: string (required) - host actor label or FName

tools.name: get_log_lines
tools.description: Read recent UE Output Log entries from the in-process ring buffer (last 1000 lines). Supports category substring filter and minimum verbosity filter.

params.count: int (optional, default 100, max 1000) - max lines to return
params.category_filter: string (optional) - case-insensitive substring filter on log category
params.min_verbosity: string (optional, default 'Log') - Fatal | Error | Warning | Display | Log | Verbose | VeryVerbose

tools.name: execute_console_command
tools.description: Execute a UE console command (e.g. 'stat fps', 'r.ScreenPercentage 50') and capture its output.

params.capture_output: bool (optional, default true) - capture and return command output

tools.name: inspect_asset
tools.description: Read everything the asset registry knows about a single asset: class, all registry tags, dependency packages, referencer packages, on-disk file size.

params.path: string (required) - asset path or package path

tools.name: move_asset
tools.description: Move an asset to a different folder; leaf name unchanged. UE auto-creates a redirector at the source path.

tools.name: rename_asset
tools.description: Rename an asset's leaf name; folder unchanged. UE auto-creates a redirector at the old name.

tools.name: duplicate_asset
tools.description: Copy an asset to a new path. Source asset is preserved; destination must not already exist. No redirector is created (callers reference the duplicate by its new path).

params.dest_path: string (required) - destination asset path (must not exist)

tools.name: delete_asset
tools.description: Delete an asset. Refuses if referenced by other packages unless force=true. Force-delete cannot be recovered via Undo.

params.force: bool (optional, default false) - delete even if referenced

tools.name: inspect_sequence
tools.description: Read structure of a Level Sequence asset: tracks, sections, bindings, frame rate, playback range.

tools.name: create_sequence
tools.description: Create a new Level Sequence asset with display frame rate and playback range.

tools.name: bind_actor_to_sequence
tools.description: Add a level actor as a possessable binding to a Level Sequence.

tools.name: create_material_instance
tools.description: Create a UMaterialInstanceConstant asset and set its parent to an existing UMaterial or UMaterialInstance.

params.parent_path: string (required) - path of the parent UMaterial or UMaterialInstance
params.name: string (required) - leaf asset name (no '/' or '.')

tools.name: set_mi_parameter
tools.description: Override a scalar/vector/texture parameter on a UMaterialInstanceConstant.

params.parameter: string (required) - parameter name as declared on the parent material
params.type: string (required) - one of: scalar | vector | texture
params.value: varies by type - scalar: number; vector: {r,g,b,a}; texture: string asset path

tools.name: inspect_material
tools.description: List parameter names declared by a UMaterial or UMaterialInstance: scalar, vector, texture, and static-switch parameters.

tools.name: inspect_material_instance
tools.description: Read a UMaterialInstanceConstant's parent and currently-overridden parameter values (scalar/vector/texture).

tools.name: run_python_file
tools.description: Execute a .py file from disk via the editor's embedded Python. Complement to execute_unreal_python; avoids JSON escaping pain for non-trivial scripts.

params.path: string (required) - filesystem path to a .py file (absolute or relative to editor CWD, typically the project root)

tools.name: fix_up_redirectors
tools.description: Cascade-update consumers of UObjectRedirector assets under a folder, then delete the redirector stubs. Cleans up after move_asset / rename_asset.

params.path: string (required) - package path under which to recursively fix up, e.g. /Game/ or /Game/Materials

tools.name: apply_python_to_selection
tools.description: Run user Python with `selection` (selected level actors) and `selected_assets` (selected content-browser assets) pre-bound as locals. Convenience wrapper around execute_unreal_python.

params.code: string (required) - Python source. `selection` and `selected_assets` are pre-bound by the injected boilerplate.

tools.name: compile_blueprint
tools.description: Explicit Blueprint recompile via FKismetEditorUtilities::CompileBlueprint. For BPs that were mutated externally (e.g. via execute_unreal_python) and need a recompile without further mutation.

params.skip_save: bool (optional, default false) - suppress Save-On-Compile auto-save

tools.name: get_console_variable
tools.description: Read a UE Console Variable by name; returns value in all four representations (string/int/float/bool) plus type, read-only flag, last-setter, and help text.

params.name: string (required) - exact CVar name, case-sensitive (e.g. r.ScreenPercentage)

tools.name: set_console_variable
tools.description: Mutate a UE Console Variable by name. Polymorphic value (string/number/bool) coerced to string and forwarded at ECVF_SetByConsole priority. Pre-rejects read-only CVars; post-verifies the change landed.

params.name: string (required) - exact CVar name, case-sensitive
params.value: string | number | bool (required) - new value; coerced to string before Set

returns.name: string
returns.note: string (only when post-set value differs from requested)

tools.name: poll_events
tools.description: Tier 2 entrypoint: drain editor events fired since the caller's last poll. UE delegates (starter set: actor_spawned, actor_deleted, asset_added) push into a 1000-entry ring buffer (FUCMCPEventBus); this handler returns the slice with seq > since_seq, capped at max_count.

params.since_seq: int (optional, default -1) - return events with seq > since_seq
params.max_count: int (optional, default 100, max 1000) - cap returned events
params.event_filter: array of string (optional) - substring filters on event type; OR-combined

tools.name: wait_for_events
tools.description: Bridge-side composition: repeatedly polls UE's poll_events until matching events arrive or timeout_ms expires. Implemented in the bridge (Python) rather than UE C++ so the wait runs in a separate process, leaving UE's game thread free to actually fire game-thread events during the wait.

params.timeout_ms: int (optional, default 500, hard cap 30000) - max wait in ms
params.poll_interval_ms: int (optional, default 100, range 25-1000) - bridge-side polling cadence
params.max_count: int (optional, default 100, max 1000) - cap returned events
params.event_filter: array of string (optional) - substring filters on event type

tools.name: register_subscription
tools.description: Create a server-side cursor + filter on FUCMCPEventBus. Returns a subscription_id pairs with poll_subscription / unsubscribe. Cursor starts at the bus's current next_seq (subscribers see events fired AFTER subscription).

params.event_filter: array of string (optional) - substring filters on event type; OR-combined

tools.name: unsubscribe
tools.description: Remove a subscription. Idempotent: unknown ids return ok=true with was_present=false, so blanket shutdown unsubscribes are safe.

tools.name: poll_subscription
tools.description: Drain events for a subscription. Per-sub cursor advances atomically -- successful poll never returns the same events twice. No since_seq / event_filter params (both are server-side state).

params.max_count: int (optional, default 100, max 1000) - cap returned events

tools.name: start_sleep_task
tools.description: PR #44 task-framework tracer: background-sleeps for duration_ms then completes. Returns task_id; poll via poll_task. Hard cap 1 hour.

tools.name: poll_task
tools.description: Read current state of a task. Non-blocking. Status: pending | running | completed | cancelled | failed.

tools.name: cancel_task
tools.description: Request cooperative cancellation. Sets atomic flag; worker observes within ~50ms and transitions to status='cancelled'. Idempotent.

tools.name: list_tasks
tools.description: Enumerate tasks in the registry with optional status/type filters and a limit. Atomic snapshot. Mirrors find_assets's total/matched/returned convention.

params.status_filter: string (optional) - one of pending | running | completed | cancelled | failed

returns.tasks: array of {task_id, type, status, start_time, end_time, cancel_requested, result?, error?}

tools.name: exec_python_persistent
tools.description: Like execute_unreal_python but state PERSISTS across calls. Implemented via FPythonCommandEx with FileExecutionScope=Public (shared globals dict with the editor's Python console). Pairs with reset_python_state.

params.code: string (required) - Python source to execute against the persistent globals dict

tools.name: reset_python_state
tools.description: Clear all user-defined names from UE Python's public globals dict. Pairs with exec_python_persistent. Names starting with '_' (dunders + conventional private) are preserved.

tools.name: find_console_variables
tools.description: Prefix-search IConsoleManager registry; returns matching CVar names + types + read-only flags. C++ handler -- direct iteration of UE's internal registry. Part of language-shim experiment (PR #46).

params.prefix: string (optional, default empty) - case-sensitive prefix filter
params.limit: int (optional, default 100, max 1000) - cap returned variables

returns.note: string (only when result count hit the cap)

tools.name: inspect_static_mesh
tools.description: Read structural properties of a UStaticMesh: LOD count, per-LOD vertex/triangle counts, bounding box, material slots. C++ handler. Part of language-shim experiment (PR #46).

params.path: string (required) - UE asset path of a UStaticMesh

tools.name: inspect_niagara_system
tools.description: Read structural properties of a UNiagaraSystem: emitters, user parameters, system-level settings. C++ handler. Calls EnsureFullyLoaded() before access (UNiagaraSystem uses LazyOnDemand load behavior).

params.path: string (required) - UE asset path of a UNiagaraSystem

returns.name: string
returns.effect_type: string (optional, when set; asset path of the UNiagaraEffectType instance)
returns.fixed_bounds: object (optional, when bFixedBounds is true) - {min, max, size, center}

tools.name: inspect_anim_blueprint
tools.description: Read structural properties of a UAnimBlueprint: parent class, target skeleton, template flag, compile status, baked state machines, anim functions (with implemented flag), sync groups, parent anim blueprint chain. C++ handler. Guards UAnimBlueprintGeneratedClass for null when blueprint hasn't been compiled.

params.path: string (required) - UE asset path of a UAnimBlueprint

returns.name: string
returns.target_skeleton: string (conditional, when bIsTemplate is false and TargetSkeleton is set; asset path)
returns.blueprint_status: string enum: UpToDate | UpToDateWithWarnings | Dirty | Error | Unknown
returns.parent_anim_blueprint: string (conditional, when this blueprint subclasses another anim blueprint; asset path)

tools.name: inspect_landscape
tools.description: Read structural properties of an ALandscape scene actor (not an asset). Component dimensions, total component count, landscape material asset path, world-space loaded bounds, GUID identity. Lookup by actor label and/or LandscapeGuid string.

params.name: string (optional) - actor label; when omitted with guid, returns the sole landscape if exactly one exists

returns.name: string
returns.landscape_material: string (conditional, when LandscapeMaterial is set; asset path)

tools.name: get_camera_transform
tools.description: Read level-editor viewport camera transform. SYNTHETIC bridge-side handler -- composes execute_unreal_python + get_log_lines via marker pattern. Part of language-shim experiment (PR #46).

tools.name: set_camera_transform
tools.description: Set level-editor viewport camera transform. SYNTHETIC bridge-side handler. Single execute_unreal_python round-trip. Part of language-shim experiment (PR #46).

params.location: object (optional) - {x, y, z}; missing fields default to 0
params.rotation: object (optional) - {pitch, yaw, roll}; missing fields default to 0

tools.name: screenshot_actor
tools.description: Frame the editor viewport on an actor and capture a focused PNG screenshot. SYNTHETIC bridge-side handler that composes focus_actor + get_viewport_screenshot. Returns base64 PNG plus the focused actor's identity and world location.

params.name: string (required) - actor label or unique name to focus on
